DECLARATION OF DIANA R. DIGENNARO FILED BY PETITIONER THE SAN FRANCISCO LAW LIBRARY
SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF MARCIA BELL FILED BY PETITIONER THE SAN FRANCISCO LAW LIBRARY

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT MAKES THE FOLLOWING DISCLOSURE: I KNOW DENIS RICE. WE PRACTICED LAW AT THE SAME LAW FIRM- WHAT WAS THEN KNOWN AS HOWARD RICE FROM DEC. 1981 TO MARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992-1995. (I SUBSEQUENTLY MOVED ON TO OTHER POSITIONS BEFORE I WAS APPOINTED TO THE BENCH IN FEB 2005.) HOWARD RICE, AS I UNDERSTAND IT, IS NOW PART OF ARNOLD & PORTER, AS OF ABOUT A YEAR AGO. JERRY FALK, ONE OF MY FORMER PARTNERS AT HOWARD RICE, RETIRED EARLIER THIS YEAR. IN JANUARY OF THIS YEAR, I WAS A GUEST AT A RETIREMENT DINNER FOR MR. FALK THAT WAS HOSTED BY WHAT IS NOW ARNOLD & PORTER, TO WHICH MANY CURRENT AND FORMER COLLEAGUES OF MR. FALK'S FROM THE OLD HOWARD RICE FIRM WERE INVITED. MR. RICE WAS AT THAT DINNER. I AM ESTIMATING THAT THERE WERE ABOUT 100 PEOPLE AT THE DINNER. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. I DO NOT BELIEVE ANYONE AWARE OF THE FACTS THAT I HAVE DISCLOSED WOULD BELIEVE THAT I COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. I DO NOT KNOW DIANA DIGENARRO, AND HAVE NOT HEARD OF HER BEFORE I SAW THESE MOVING PAPERS. (NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED.) RULING: THE HEARING ON PETITIONER THE SAN FRANCISCO LAW LIBRARY'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 24, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. PETITIONER TO FILE/SERVE BRIEF NO LATER THAN 5/17/13; RESPONDENT TO FILE REPLY BY 5/21/13. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-05-2013 Continued To May-24-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: denis t. rice, esq., and diana d. digennaro, esq., 415.471.3177 and 415.471.3111, for petitioner the san francisco law library.
cristine van aken, deputy city attorney, and andrew n. shen, deputy city attorney, 415.554.4700, for respondent city and county of san francisco.

AFTER CASE CALLED, THE COURT MAKES THE FOLLOWING DISCLOSURE: i KNOW DENIS RICE. WE PRACTICED LAW AT THE SAME LAW FIRM- WHAT WAS THEN KNOWN AS HOWARD RICE FROM dEC. 1981 TO mARCH 1987, AND FROM 1992-1995. (i SUBSEQUENTLY MOVED ON TO OTHER POSITIONS BEFORE i WAS APPOINTED TO THE BENCH IN fEB 2005.) HOWARD RICE, AS I UNDERSTAND IT, IS NOW PART OF ARNOLD & PORTER, AS OF ABOUT A YEAR AGO. JERRY FALK, ONE OF MY FORMER PARTNERS AT HOWARD RICE, RETIRED EARLIER THIS YEAR. IN JANUARY OF THIS YEAR, i WAS A GUEST AT A RETIREMENT DINNER FOR MR. FALK THAT WAS HOSTED BY WHAT IS NOW ARNOLD & PORTER, TO WHICH MANY CURRENT AND FORMER COLLEAGUES OF MR. FALK'S FROM THE OLD HOWARD RICE FIRM WERE INVITED. MR. RICE WAS AT THAT DINNER. I AM ESTIMATING THAT THERE WERE ABOUT 100 PEOPLE AT THE DINNER. THIS WILL NOT AFFECT MY ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. I DO NOT BELIEVE ANYONE AWARE OF THE FACTS THAT I HAVE DISCLOSED WOULD BELIEVE THAT i COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. I DO NOT KNOW DIANA DIGENARRO, AND HAVE NOT HEARD OF HER BEFORE i SAW THESE MOVING PAPERS.

RULING: Argued and the Court CONTINUED THE HEARING ON PETITIONER THE SAN FRANCISCO LAW LIBRARY's Motion For Preliminary Injunction TO MAY 24, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. PETITIONER TO FILE/SERVE BRIEF NO LATER THAN 5/17/13; RESPONDENT TO FILE REPLY BY 5/21/13. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
